title: Tactile-Driven Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Framework for Dexterous Robotic Manipulation
abstract: To address the challenges of robotic dexterous manipulation in complex environments, this paper proposes a hierarchical reinforcement learning (HRL) framework driven by tactile sensing. The high-level policy selects manipulation sub-skills based on visual and language inputs, while the low-level policy executes fine-grained force control guided primarily by tactile feedback.
keyInsights: 触觉信号作为低层策略的主要感知模态，在接触丰富任务中显著优于纯视觉方案
层次化设计实现高层语义规划(what to do)与低层力控制(how to do)的关注点分离
触觉时空编码器将高维触觉阵列压缩为紧凑特征向量
在插拔、旋拧、柔性物体操作等接触丰富场景中验证有效性
为触觉传感器在实际操作中的系统化应用提供了训练范式
methodology: The framework consists of two levels: (1) High-level Policy: a VLM-based planner receives visual observation and language instruction, outputting a discrete sub-skill selection (grasp, rotate, insert, etc.); (2) Low-level Policy: a tactile-driven RL controller receives encoded tactile signals (spatial-temporal features from a tactile encoder) and executes precise force-position control. The tactile encoder uses a 1D-CNN + LSTM architecture to capture both spatial distribution and temporal dynamics of contact forces. Training uses a hierarchical curriculum where the low-level policy is first trained on individual sub-skills, then the high-level policy learns to compose them.
impact: 为触觉传感器在灵巧操作中的应用提供了系统化的层次化RL训练框架
